notes: build annotated h5bench with dftracer, smoke test h5bench_write

attempts.error: annotated/source/ is missing the actual h5bench codebase (no CMakeLists.txt, no src/); it only contains stray scripts/traces/record subdirs. STEP 2 annotation output is incomplete/blocked upstream — cannot build.

Build succeeded (all 7 binaries built, correctly linked against session HDF5 1.14.5 and libdftracer_core), but functional smoke test reveals a runtime ABI mismatch: brahma's prebuilt HDF5 interception wrapper (libbrahma.so.1, shipped in pydftracer 2.0.3.dev52) was compiled against an older H5Fcreate_async/H5Dwrite_async signature lacking the app_file/app_func/app_line async-debug prologue args that HDF5 1.14.5 added. Calling h5bench_write against the session's HDF5 1.14.5 corrupts hid_t args passed through brahma's interposed H5Fcreate/H5Dcreate/H5Dwrite/H5Dclose calls -- HDF5 emits H5Fcreate_async 'not a file create property list' diagnostics and the .h5 output file is never created (0 bytes / missing), even though the process exits 0 and prints a fabricated-looking success summary. Confirmed by diff: unannotated baseline binary with the identical config produces a real 67MB .h5 file; annotated binary produces none.
